concerns.text: Task T015 implements the 'valid logging hours <80%' check but omits the explicit logic for 'any single data gap >20% of total session time' required by FR-007 and US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3. This is a semantic weakening of the exclusion criteria.

Task T019 schedules the power analysis justification in Phase 2 (Foundational), but FR-008 explicitly requires this justification 'prior to initiating data collection'. Since Phase 3 (Data Collection/Synthesis) begins immediately after, the task ordering fails to enforce the temporal constraint of the requirement.

FR-001 mandates aggregating continuous logs into three discrete categories (Low/Moderate/High) for 'descriptive analysis only'. The tasks in Phase 4 (T024-T027) focus exclusively on the continuous model and post-hoc tests, with no task defined to generate the required descriptive statistics or summary table for these categories.

The spec's Edge Cases explicitly require handling for participants in a 'completely silent environment (0dB)' by treating them as 'Low' noise and checking for model singularity. Task T016 only addresses RT outliers; there is no task to implement the 0dB handling logic or singularity checks.

SC-003 requires measuring the 'stability of the Moderate noise effect size' specifically. Task T035 only mentions reporting 'effect sizes' generally and 'false-positive rate variation', lacking the explicit instruction to isolate and report the stability of the 'Moderate' category as mandated.
